pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 28-29 creates customer with first_name='Sarah' and last_name='Jones' via FreeScout ORM
setup_task.sh line 71 creates conversation with subject 'Suspicious invoice attached - urgent check needed'. Keyword is present as substring.
export_result.sh line 13 queries for tags WHERE name = 'Security Alert'. This is the tag the task requires users to create.

export_result.sh line 45 checks if internal notes contain 'header analysis' as a case-insensitive substring. Task description (task.json line 6) requires adding note with 'Escalated for immediate header analysis.'

Verified across multiple sources: (1) export_result.sh line 30 queries users WHERE email='admin@helpdesk.local'; (2) docker-compose.yml line 32 sets ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@helpdesk.local; (3) setup_freescout.sh line 207 displays 'Admin email: admin@helpdesk.local'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses fully synthetic data created by the setup_task.sh script. The target customer is 'Sarah Jones' with email 'sarah.jones@marketing-partner.net'. The target conversation has subject 'Suspicious invoice attached - urgent check needed' which contains the keyword 'Suspicious invoice'. The task requires users to create a tag named 'Security Alert', assign the conversation to admin user with email 'admin@helpdesk.local', and add an internal note containing the text 'header analysis'. The system is FreeScout, a help desk application running in Docker, with data stored in MariaDB. Three distractor conversations are created to test whether users correctly identify and tag only the target conversation.
